metadata.title: Jema'a Local Government
metadata.description: Official website of Jema'a Local Government Area, Kaduna State, Nigeria.

slides.title: Jema'a – The Heart of Southern Kaduna
slides.subtitle: Jema'a Local Government Area is an important economic and cultural center in southern Kaduna, with Kafanchan serving as a major railway and trading hub.

slides.title: Jema'a – A Land of Culture and Agriculture
slides.subtitle: Known for fertile lands and vibrant communities, Jema'a contributes significantly to agricultural production in Kaduna State.

paragraphs: Jema'a Local Government Area is located in the southern region of Kaduna State, Nigeria. The administrative headquarters is Kafanchan, one of the most important towns in southern Kaduna.
The LGA is home to several ethnic groups including the Fantswam people, Hausa, Fulani, and other communities that have lived in the region for generations.
Agriculture and trade are the main economic activities, with crops such as maize, ginger, millet, and sorghum widely cultivated across the area.

chairman.name: Executive Chairman, Jema'a Local Government
messageParagraphs: Welcome to the official website of Jema'a Local Government Area. Our administration is committed to improving infrastructure, education, healthcare, and agricultural productivity across our communities.
Jema'a is known for its cultural diversity, hardworking farmers, and vibrant markets that contribute to the economic growth of Kaduna State.
Through good governance and community participation, we continue to pursue sustainable development that benefits every resident.

cards.title: Maize Farming
cards.subtitle: Staple Crop
cards.description: Maize is widely cultivated across Jema'a communities and serves as a staple food and major agricultural product.

cards.title: Ginger Farming
cards.subtitle: Major Cash Crop
cards.description: Southern Kaduna is famous for ginger production, and farmers in Jema'a cultivate ginger as an important cash crop.

cards.title: Livestock Rearing
cards.subtitle: Animal Husbandry
cards.description: Livestock such as cattle, goats, sheep, and poultry are raised across rural communities within Jema'a LGA.

news.subtitle: Stay informed about development projects, community programs, and government initiatives in Jema'a.

footer.headquarters: Headquarters: Local Government Secretariat, Kafanchan, Kaduna State, Nigeria.

hero.title: About Jema'a
hero.subtitle: Discover the culture, geography, and economy of Jema'a Local Government Area.

location: Neighboring LGAs: Jaba, Kaura, Sanga, and Zangon Kataf.
demographics: Major Ethnic Groups: Fantswam, Hausa, Fulani.
Languages Spoken: Fantswam, Hausa, and English.
economy: Main Economic Activities: Farming, trading, livestock rearing.
Climate: Tropical savannah climate suitable for crop farming.

geographyEconomy.title: Geography & Economy
geographyEconomy.location: Jema'a lies within the southern Kaduna region and is known for fertile agricultural land and vibrant commercial activity.
geographyEconomy.climate: The area experiences a rainy season from May to October and a dry season from November to April.
mainActivities: Crop Farming: Maize, millet, sorghum, ginger, and groundnuts.
Livestock Rearing: Cattle, goats, sheep, and poultry.
Trading: Kafanchan serves as a major commercial and railway town.

hero.title: Contact Us
hero.subtitle: Reach out to Jema'a Local Government for inquiries and information.

contactDetails.text: Jema'a Local Government Secretariat, Kafanchan, Kaduna State, Nigeria

contactPage.successMessage: Thank you for contacting Jema'a Local Government. We will get back to you soon.
